chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Civil Transfer Application No.8 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES Hearing of Case 1. For hearing of main case. 2. For hearing of CMA No.570/2025. 04.03.2025 Ms. Ghazala Illahi, Advocate for the Applicant. Mr. Muhammad Khalid Hayat, advocate for the respondent..-.-.-.-.-. Pursuant to Court notice, Mr. Muhammad Khalid Hayat, has shown appearance, files his vakalatnama and requests for time to file reply. Instant Transfer application has been moved by the applicant. I have heard the learned the learned counsel for the applicant and she has very categorically stated that the learned trial Court is passing orders, in which the Applicant is directed to pay exorbitant sums of money as maintenance. Moreover, the learned counsel has stated that the learned Trial Court is passing repeated orders in which the quantum of the maintenance granted is excessive. She has also stated that the applicant earns Rs.50,000/- per month and is unable to comply with the said order. 2. Conversely, learned counsel for the respondent has stated that no specific ground has been alleged for transfer of the case and the applicant is only avoiding their obligation to maintain the minors. He also stated that the orders for maintenance are reasonable and the applicant ought to maintain the minor pursuant to the said order. 3. Heard the parties at some length. I specifically inquired from the learned counsel for the applicant, the ground on which she has sought the transfer of this case and replied she has repeatedly invited my attention to various orders of the learned trial Court, which according to her, are excessive and exorbitant.

Heard the parties at some length. I specifically inquired from the learned counsel for the applicant, the ground on which she has sought the transfer of this case and replied she has repeatedly invited my attention to various orders of the learned trial Court, which according to her, are excessive and exorbitant. The said ground being legal in nature, learned counsel for the applicant may have the remedy available under the law, which according to her, she has already sought in Const. Petition No.83/2025. No specific allegation of bias on any other ground, which may deprive her from fair trial, has been made by the learned counsel. Accordingly, this transfer application being devoid of any merits is hereby dismissed with no order as to costs. JUDGE Nadeem
